title: 帷幕
summary:
In a letter to Hastings, Hercule Poirot reveals that he has been feigning paralysis at Styles Manor to deceive Curtis. Poirot explains that he intentionally sent George away and replaced him with less experienced staff because George’s astute judgment would have discovered that Poirot’s health had actually improved following his return from Egypt. Poirot also admits to witnessing Hastings attempting to kill Alton by tampering with the man's sleeping pills. To prevent the crime, Poirot observed Hastings through a bathroom keyhole and subsequently used his own sleeping pills to ensure Hastings fell asleep after drinking hot chocolate, thereby neutralizing the threat.

Poirot expresses concern that Norton’s manipulative schemes may eventually target Hastings' daughter. He then reveals the truth regarding Barbara Franklin's death, asserting that Hastings was the unwitting cause of the tragedy. Poirot explains that Mrs. Franklin was an ambitious woman who desired to marry the wealthy Boyd Carrington. To achieve this, she planned to have her husband, John Franklin, die of poisoned aconite under the guise of a scientific experiment. However, Barbara felt threatened by the social dynamics involving the Crewe nurse and decided to act prematurely to become a widow.

During a moment when the household went outside to view a shooting star, Mrs. Franklin prepared coffee using a rotating table. While Hastings remained behind to work on a crossword puzzle, the coffee was switched: Mrs. Franklin accidentally consumed the strychnine intended for John, while John drank the coffee intended for her. Poirot admits that although he realized the truth, he allowed the death to be framed as a suicide to protect the innocence of John Franklin and Judith.
